etymology.description: semantic 广 (guǎng, "building") + phonetic 付 (fù, "to pay") -> a type of building
etymology.interpretation: The character 府(fǔ, "government office") combines 广 (guǎng, "building") to suggest a structure or place, with 付 (fù, "to pay") providing the sound. It historically referred to a building or place associated with administrative functions, leading to the sense of a government office.

examples.chinese: 新建的政府大楼看起来很现代化(新建的政府大樓看起來很現代化)。
examples.pinyin: Xīnjiàn de zhèngfǔ dàlóu kàn qǐlái hěn xiàndàihuà.
examples.english: The newly built government office building looks very modern.

examples.chinese: 政府正在制定新的政策(政府正在制定新的政策)，以应对城市发展(以應對城市發展)。
examples.pinyin: Zhèngfǔ zhèngzài zhìdìng xīn de zhèngcè, yǐ yìngduì chéngshì fāzhǎn.
examples.english: The government is formulating new policies to address urban development.

examples.chinese: 市政府计划改善交通设施(市政府計劃改善交通設施)。
examples.pinyin: Shì zhèngfǔ jìhuà gǎishàn jiāotōng shèshī.
examples.english: The city government plans to improve transportation facilities.
